connections.name: Meta Air Link
requirements: Router in same room as play space

connections.name: Virtual Desktop
requirements: Virtual Desktop app purchased on Quest Store

knownIssues.title: Quest Link shader compilation stutters
knownIssues.description: First-time use of Quest Link causes shader compilation stutters in SteamVR games. Resolves after initial caching.
knownIssues.workaround: Let SteamVR finish shader pre-caching on first launch. Avoid clearing shader cache unnecessarily.

knownIssues.title: Air Link bitrate drops on congested Wi-Fi
knownIssues.description: Air Link automatically reduces bitrate when Wi-Fi congestion is detected, causing visible compression artifacts.
knownIssues.workaround: Use a dedicated 5GHz SSID for the Quest. Move router closer to play space. Consider Wi-Fi 6E router.

knownIssues.title: Meta Quest software updates can regress performance
knownIssues.description: Some Quest firmware updates have introduced Link and Air Link performance regressions. Check Meta forums after updates.
knownIssues.workaround: If performance degrades after an update, check r/OculusQuest and Meta forums for known issues.

optimizationTips.tip: Use Oculus OpenXR runtime instead of SteamVR for native Quest games
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Using Meta's own VR software instead of SteamVR can make games run smoother because it cuts out a middleman.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: The Oculus OpenXR runtime has lower overhead than the SteamVR runtime for Quest Link/Air Link. Set Oculus as the active OpenXR runtime in Oculus PC App settings for 5-15% frame time improvement in OpenXR titles.

optimizationTips.tip: Enable Wi-Fi 6 (802.11ax) and use 5GHz or 6GHz band
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Make sure your router is using the fastest Wi-Fi band available. This reduces the delay between your movements and what you see in VR.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: Wi-Fi 6 (802.11ax) on 5GHz provides up to 1.2Gbps throughput with lower latency than Wi-Fi 5. Use a dedicated SSID on a non-DFS 5GHz channel (36, 40, 44, 48) or Wi-Fi 6E on 6GHz for zero congestion. Set channel width to 80MHz minimum.

optimizationTips.tip: Set Link encoding bitrate to 200-300 Mbps for best clarity
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Increasing the streaming quality in the Oculus app makes the picture sharper, but needs a good GPU and connection.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: In Oculus Debug Tool or Virtual Desktop settings, increase encode bitrate to 200-300 Mbps. This reduces compression artifacts at the cost of higher GPU encoder load and network bandwidth. Requires NVENC/AMF hardware encoder headroom and stable 5GHz link.
